title: Missed Out on the AI Memory Rally? These 3 Stocks Are Just Getting Started.
author: Justin Pope
description: As AI demand for memory chips creates a supercycle, three semiconductor-related stocks offer alternatives to already-surged memory chip makers. Rambus licenses high-speed memory interface IP, Lam Research manufactures semiconductor equipment, and Teradyne provides testing systems—all positioned to benefit from years of continued AI-driven memory demand growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company licenses high-speed memory interface IP to major memory producers; positioned to benefit from agentic AI growth; analysts project 19% annual earnings growth over 3-5 years despite current 48x P/E valuation.

Sells semiconductor manufacturing equipment; global memory market expected to grow from $171B (2025) to $447B (2034); memory represents 39% of revenue; analysts project 21% annual earnings growth with room for upside despite 68x P/E valuation.

Provides critical testing systems for complex HBM chips; revenue grew 87% YoY with AI driving 70% of revenue; projected 70% revenue growth over two years; analysts estimate 34% annual earnings growth, justifying 61x P/E valuation.

Memory chip stock that has already surged ~800% in past year; article suggests it has already experienced parabolic price movements, making it less attractive for new investors entering the AI memory rally.

title: The Nasdaq-100 Is Getting 5 New Members. Here's the List.
author: Patrick Sanders
description: The Nasdaq-100 index is adding five new members on June 22, 2026: Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Teradyne, and Rocket Lab. Four of these companies operate in AI infrastructure, networking, and computing capacity, while Rocket Lab is a space platform company. All five are significantly outperforming the market with year-to-date gains ranging from 53% to 242%, reflecting broader market trends favoring AI and infrastructure companies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue growth of 93% year-over-year to $308.4M in Q1, with net income more than doubling. Company is a leading provider of AI connectivity hardware with growing demand for its Scorpio X-Series products.
Massive $100B revenue backlog, major contracts with Meta ($35B) and Microsoft, surpassed 1 GW of active power with plans to reach 8 GW by 2030. Strong market position in AI cloud computing capacity.

Best performer on the list with 242% year-to-date gains. Exceeded capacity targets (3.5 GW vs 3 GW goal), received $2B investment from Nvidia, and has $46B in collective deals with Microsoft and Meta.
Q1 revenue nearly doubled to $1.28B with AI-related demand accounting for over 70% of revenue. Strong earnings per share growth and positive guidance for continued AI-driven momentum.
Q1 revenue grew 63% to $200.3M with record backlog of $2.2B (up 20% quarter-over-quarter). Strategic acquisition of Motiv Space Systems strengthens capabilities in space robotics.
Mentioned as a major customer committing significant capital ($35B to CoreWeave, $46B collective with Nebius) for AI infrastructure, but no direct company performance data provided.
Mentioned as a major customer with significant contracts ($46B collective with Nebius) for AI computing capacity, but no direct company performance data provided.
Mentioned as providing computing platforms and making a $2B investment in Nebius, indicating strong position in AI infrastructure ecosystem, but no direct performance metrics provided.

title: Nasdaq-100 Index® June 2026 Quarterly Changes
author: Nasdaq
description: Nasdaq announced the results of its June 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, effective June 22, 2026. Five companies were added to the index: Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. Five companies were removed: Charter Communications, Cognizant Technology Solutions, Insmed, Verisk Analytics, and Zscaler.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Added to the Nasdaq-100 Index, indicating recognition as one of the largest Nasdaq-listed non-financial companies

title: Roblox, Spotify, And Robinhood Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (April 27-May 1): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Multiple large-cap stocks experienced significant declines last week due to earnings misses and disappointing guidance. The top losers included Rambus (down 28.47%), Summit Therapeutics (down 27.82%), and Teradyne (down 17.85%), with notable declines also from Roblox, Spotify, and Robinhood. Weak financial results and lowered forward guidance across tech, fintech, and other sectors shook investor confidence.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 28.47% after reporting worse-than-expected Q1 adjusted EPS results
Stock fell 27.82% following Q1 financial results announcement
Stock declined 17.85% after reporting Q1 financial results
Stock fell 14.84% due to worse-than-expected Q1 sales results
Stock dropped 13.13% after mixed Q1 results and Q2 guidance below estimates

Stock fell 12.61% due to worse-than-expected Q1 results and multiple analyst price target reductions
Stock slumped 12.08% after reporting worse-than-expected Q1 sales results

title: Here's Why This AI-Related Company's Stock Soared Today
author: Lee Samaha
description: Teradyne's stock surged over 15% as investors viewed a recent dip as a buying opportunity. The company reported that AI now drives 70% of its revenue (up from 60%), with 87% year-over-year revenue growth. However, second-quarter guidance disappointed with a sequential revenue decline expected, though analysts from Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan maintained positive outlooks, citing natural order timing lumpiness rather than fundamental weakness.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 13.6% on positive analyst sentiment despite disappointing Q2 guidance. Strong AI revenue growth (70% of total, 87% YoY), with Goldman Sachs raising price target to $350 and JPMorgan upgrading to overweight. Market treated the dip as a buying opportunity.
Mentioned as analyst providing positive coverage on Teradyne with price target raise to $350 and buy rating. No direct impact on Goldman Sachs itself reported.
